metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert the security-invariant audit ACs in Phase 1 and Phase 2 to observable end-states by naming a concrete output artifact for each (e.g., 'a dedicated section in ADR-0042 documents the GerriScary findings and mitigations, checked into the repo as part of this PR' and 'an integration test asserts the bot re-verifies is:submittable immediately before submit').
findings.finding: The security-invariant audit ACs in both Phase 1 and Phase 2 are effort-framed ('re-verify', 'audit', 'GerriScary review') with no specified output artifact, making it impossible to determine in-session when the criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Security-invariant audit bullet: 'Security-invariant audit: re-verify is:submittable … CVE-2025-1568 / GerriScary review'; Phase 2 AC — 'Gate-touching part (its own security-invariant-audit AC)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Phase 1 security AC: 'ADR-0042 includes a GerriScary/CVE-2025-1568 review section listing each attack vector and its mitigation; a unit/integration test asserts that the submit path calls is:submittable on the exact rebase-tip SHA, not an earlier SHA.' Add analogous artifact language to Phase 2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to S3 (or S4) to have the bot record a `review_failed` outcome event when LLM-Review -1 is observed, so the bot is the authoritative source for all four outcome types — not just two.
findings.finding: The `review_failed` outcome in the typed enum has no bot-recorded source in S3 (failure handling); S4 explicitly falls back to native Gerrit labels for this case, meaning the bot is NOT the single source of truth for `review_failed` — contradicting the parent AC 'The bot is the single source of truth for the outcome.'
findings.suggested_fix: In S3, add: 'LLM-Review -1: remove Autosubmit and record a review_failed outcome event (bot-originated, not synthesized by the command from native labels).' Update S4 to remove the fallback-to-native-labels language for review_failed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate one Phase 1 child (recommend S2 or a new S-security story) to own a holistic Phase 1 security-invariant audit covering the full CVE-2025-1568/GerriScary checklist and produce a documented audit artifact.
findings.finding: The Phase 1 security-invariant audit (CVE-2025-1568 / GerriScary review) — a single composite parent AC — has no single owning child that delivers it as a unified Phase 1 deliverable. S1 audits only the label ACL scope; S2 mechanically implements re-verify-before-submit; S8 explicitly owns the security-invariant audit only for Phase 2. No child commits to a holistic Phase 1 GerriScary posture audit tying together the full 'two-vote gate is not weakened across the whole Phase 1 system' argument.
findings.location: child 843b-801f-9ef9-442d (S1) and child 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec (S2) — Phase 1 AC8 split across both without a unifying owner
findings.suggested_fix: Add a security-invariant audit AC to S2 (or extract a dedicated Phase 1 security story) with: 'Produces a written Phase 1 GerriScary posture audit covering (a) Autosubmit ACL cannot forge LLM-Review or Verified votes, (b) re-verify-is:submittable race window, (c) CVE-2025-1568 applicability; outcome recorded in ADR-0042 or a linked doc.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Phase 2 AC requiring the auto-lander to emit a structured observability artifact (e.g., a log line or GHA step summary recording `batch_size`, `ci_runs_saved`, outcome) so the 'N changes → 1 CI cycle' promise is end-state-verifiable.
findings.finding: The success criterion 'redundant CI runs from the parallel-landing race are eliminated; under bursts, a batch of N non-conflicting changes costs ~1 CI wall-clock cycle (Phase 2)' has no corresponding AC that makes it end-state-observable. Phase 2 ACs specify the batching mechanism but define no observability hook (log entry, metric, or dashboard) to validate the claimed CI-cycle reduction actually holds.
findings.location: Success Criteria (fifth bullet) and Phase 2 ACs
findings.suggested_fix: In Phase 2 ACs, add: 'The bot emits a structured log entry per batch recording `batch_size=N`, `ci_wall_clock_cycles=1`, and per-stack outcome; this log is queryable from existing CloudWatch monitoring and serves as the observable proof that the wall-clock efficiency claim holds.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the exact Gerrit group(s) permitted to vote `Autosubmit` in the Phase 1 AC (and the corresponding `project.config` addition), preferring a dedicated group scoped to the lander bot rather than the broad `Service Users` group.
findings.finding: The `Autosubmit` label's 'bot-only vote ACL' does not name the specific Gerrit group (e.g., `Service Users`, a new dedicated group, or `Administrators`), leaving the access posture for who may set the landing-intent label ambiguous — an undeclared posture on a security-relevant label.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — '`Autosubmit` Gerrit label … bot-only vote ACL, non-gating … non-sticky'; `infra/gerrit/project.config` (new label to be added)
findings.suggested_fix: Use the lander bot's dedicated group (recommended in Finding 2 above) as the sole grantee: `label-Autosubmit = +1 group lander-bot` — keeping the review-bot and CI voter out of the landing-intent path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit Phase 2 rollback / back-out plan: specify how to revert the bot's `Verified`-vote ACL, how to disable Phase 2 batching without disabling Phase 1 serial landing, and what manual remediation looks like if a bad batch lands.
findings.finding: Phase 2 introduces bot-cast `Verified` votes (a genuine gate-semantics change), but no rollback/back-out path is described: if the batch-CI bot is misconfigured or misbehaves, there is no specified mechanism to revoke or disable its Verified-vote authority without a config change that itself has no documented procedure.
findings.location: Phase 2 AC: 'Gate-touching part (its own security-invariant-audit AC): the bot casts `Verified` on batch members from the single combined-tree run'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Phase 2 AC: 'Rollback path: the bot's Verified-vote ACL is granted in a separate Gerrit group from its submit ACL; removing the group membership reverts to Phase 1 serial behavior without service restart. Document this in the ops runbook.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Phase 1 inner-loop retry spec: when submit() is rejected because main advanced (non-409, non-CI-fail — FFO rejection after Verified +1), the bot must automatically restart from the rebase step rather than surfacing `needs_rebase` to the agent; specify the max-retry count or circuit-breaker after which it does hand back.
findings.finding: The Phase 1 auto-lander loop does not specify behavior when `main` advances while CI is running — the most common concurrent-landing race. After a clean bot rebase and CI passing (Verified +1 cast), another change lands on `main`, making the change non-submittable before the bot can call submit. The plan specifies handling for CI-fail (`409` rebase conflict) and CI failure, but is silent on this inner-loop retry: the bot must restart from the rebase step, but without specifying this path the implementation could either deadlock (waiting on a submit that will never succeed) or incorrectly surface `needs_rebase` to the agent for a situation that is auto-recoverable within the bot.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'for the front Autosubmit+submittable change/chain it rebases to the current tip … → awaits a fresh Verified +1 → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Phase 1 AC: 'FFO rejection at submit time (main advanced during CI): bot re-enters the rebase→await-CI→submit loop automatically; after N consecutive FFO rejections on a non-conflicting change, surface a `needs_rebase` outcome to the agent with a note that the queue is contended.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the Gerrit API call sequence for combined-chain assembly in Phase 2 (which rebase endpoints, topic assignment, Change-Id/uploader ownership handling), or add an explicit spike AC to validate combined-chain construction and ancestor-atomic submit across stacks with distinct uploaders before committing to this mechanism.
findings.finding: Phase 2's core batching mechanism — assembling independent stacks from different uploaders into 'one combined chain' via Gerrit's relation-chain and rebase API — is asserted as feasible ('Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains') but the Gerrit-specific API operations to construct this combined chain are unspecified and non-trivial. Bors/Graphite operate on GitHub PRs, not Gerrit relation chains. Gerrit's relation-chain ownership model ties each change to its uploader, and rebasing Stack B's changes on top of Stack A's changes `on_behalf_of_uploader` creates a combined chain where the Gerrit-level parent/child relationships, Change-Id ownership, and `submitWholeTopic` scope all interact in ways not currently exercised or documented in this codebase.
findings.location: Phase 2 AC — 'Cross-stack batching: assemble queued changes/stacks into one combined chain, run CI once on the tip, land them together if green (Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to S7 (or define an interface contract between S7 and S8) stating that the batch atomic submit MUST be preceded by S8's Verified +1 casts on all batch members, with S8 listed as a hard dependency.
findings.finding: S7 (batching assembly) has a dependency gap: none of its ACs require that Verified votes exist on all batch members before issuing the atomic batch submit, yet Verified-casting (S8) is explicitly scoped as a 'separate story.' This means S7 can be implemented and declared complete without the Verified precondition check, risking a batch submit attempt while Verified is absent on some members.
findings.location: child 0128-9a17-79db-450e (S7) — AC3 and Scope statement
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S7 AC3: 'Before issuing ancestor-atomic submit, confirms that Verified +1 has been cast on all batch members by the bot (per S8); re-verifies is:submittable on the combined tip immediately before submit.' Add 'Depends on S8 (Verified-casting)' to S7's Scope.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a decision table (Gerrit primitive → outcome enum value) to the Phase 1 AC or ADR-0042, covering exactly which label values / HTTP status codes / submit-requirement states produce each of `merged`, `needs_rebase`, `ci_failed`, and `review_failed`, including precedence when multiple signals fire simultaneously.
findings.finding: The outcome enum `merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed` is named but its value vocabulary is never fully defined: specifically, no decision rule specifies which Gerrit label values or API responses the bot maps to each variant (e.g., which primitive(s) produce `review_failed` vs. `ci_failed`, and which `needs_rebase` vs. `review_failed` when a rebase fails after an LLM-Review block).
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Agent landing contract (the lead story)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small mapping table to ADR-0042: e.g., 409 on /rebase → `needs_rebase`; is:submittable polling returns LLM-Review≠MAX → `review_failed`; Verified=-1 (and LLM-Review=MAX) → `ci_failed`; change status=MERGED → `merged`. State explicit precedence: `needs_rebase` > `review_failed` > `ci_failed`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a culprit-identification algorithm to the Phase 2 batch-failure AC (e.g., binary-search bisect, sequential per-stack CI re-run, or a stated delegation to a future story), and specify the fallback when no isolated stack reproduces the failure.
findings.finding: The Phase 2 batch-failure attribution protocol references 'stack-granular attribution' and 're-run CI on the isolated culprit stack for a direct -1' but provides no decision rule for how the bot identifies which stack in the combined relation chain caused a CI failure — the schema for what 'culprit stack' means computationally is absent.
findings.location: Phase 2 AC — 'Batch failure: stack-granular attribution' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the lander bot's service account identity, its SSM parameter slot (e.g., `/rebar/prod/gerrit-lander-token`), a provisioning script reference (analogous to `service-user.sh`), and its independent rotation policy in the Phase 1 AC or a linked infra section.
findings.finding: The lander bot is a new service account with its own Gerrit HTTP token (and potentially SSH key), but the plan does not name an SSM parameter slot for this credential, reference a provisioning script (analogous to `service-user.sh`), or describe a token-rotation policy — the credential lifecycle for the boundary-crossing authentication is undeclared.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service (project infra; deploys like the review-bot — infra/compose/Dockerfile.* + compose)'; Context section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or define 'wipChain' in the Phase 1 serial auto-lander AC with an explicit description of the concurrency model (e.g., 'single-worker queue: the bot processes at most one chain at a time, with subsequent Autosubmit changes queued in FIFO order').
findings.finding: The term 'wipChain' is used without definition ('Single wipChain (serialized, one instance)'), and does not correspond to standard Gerrit or common infrastructure terminology, leaving an executor uncertain whether it refers to a code-level mutex, a Gerrit WIP-state marker, or something else.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'Single wipChain (serialized, one instance)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence clarification in the Context or a short glossary entry establishing whether 'stack' and 'chain' are exact synonyms in this plan (or define their relationship if they differ), so the atomicity invariant is unambiguous.
findings.finding: 'Stack' (agent/human perspective) and 'chain' (Gerrit relation-chain perspective) are used interchangeably throughout the plan without being declared synonymous, creating terminology drift that could mislead implementers about whether the two concepts are identical or subtly different.
findings.location: Context ('whole stack is handed back'), Phase 1 AC ('change/chain', 'rebase:chain', 'stack handed back to its owning agent'), Phase 2 AC ('Gerrit relation chains', 'culprit stack', 'innocent stacks')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Context: 'Throughout this plan, "stack" (agent terminology) and "relation chain" (Gerrit terminology) are used as synonyms: a stack is exactly a Gerrit relation chain owned by a single agent session.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document a fallback / degraded-mode procedure (or retain a collapsed 'manual fallback' section in CLAUDE.md / CONTRIBUTING.md) covering how contributors land changes if the auto-lander service is unavailable.
findings.finding: The plan rewrites CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md landing sections (removing the manual rebase-retry / watch-votes workflow), but does not describe how to restore those docs if the auto-lander service is rolled back; the old workflow instructions will have been overwritten with no recovery documented.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC: 'Docs: rewrite this repo's landing section (CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md) to the new contract'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a collapsible or appendix section in the rewritten CLAUDE.md: 'If the auto-lander is unavailable: [manual rebase-retry steps]'. This satisfies expand-contract without bloating the happy path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Phase 2 prerequisite spike: manually or via a throwaway script, assemble two real (multi-uploader) stacks into a combined Gerrit relation chain and verify ancestor-atomic submit + 409 conflict propagation behavior matches Phase 2's design assumptions before committing to the batching implementation.
findings.finding: Phase 2's core batching mechanic — assembling multiple independent stacks into a single combined Gerrit relation chain, running one CI pass on the tip, then doing an ancestor-atomic submit of the full combined chain — is asserted as feasible (described as the 'Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains') but no spike or proof-of-concept is planned. This pattern is well-established on GitHub (bors, Graphite) but Gerrit's relation-chain semantics (ownership, rebasing, submit ordering, 409-conflict propagation across stacks from different uploaders) differ materially, and the correctness of combined-chain ancestor-atomic submit across stacks with distinct uploaders is not self-evidently proven from the Gerrit API documentation alone.
findings.location: Phase 2 — batching AC: 'assemble queued changes/stacks into one combined chain, run CI once on the tip, land them together if green'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a spike task at the top of Phase 2: 'Spike: validate combined multi-stack Gerrit relation-chain submit' — create two 2-change stacks from different uploaders in a sandbox Gerrit instance, merge them into one relation chain via rebase, verify (a) `POST /changes/{id}/submit` is truly ancestor-atomic across the combined chain, (b) `on_behalf_of_uploader` works for changes not owned by the bot, (c) a 409 on one stack's change correctly isolates only that stack. Gate Phase 2 implementation on spike findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that Gerrit 3.14.1's `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` endpoint supports `on_behalf_of_uploader` (confirm exact parameter name, required ACL grants for the bot service-user account, and that it preserves `Signed-off-by` trailers under `requireSignedOffBy = true`); add a citation or a small spike result to the Phase 1 AC.
findings.finding: Phase 1 AC asserts that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (and `/rebase:chain`) supports an `on_behalf_of_uploader` option to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers when the bot rebases on behalf of the original uploader, but the plan provides no citation or version-check confirming this parameter exists and behaves as required in Gerrit 3.14.1. The parameter name, required ACL grant for the bot service account, and its interaction with DCO (`requireSignedOffBy = true` in project.config) are all unverified.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC: 'rebases to the current tip (POST /changes/{id}/rebase / /rebase:chain, on_behalf_of_uploader to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC sub-bullet: 'Spike: confirm `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` `on_behalf_of_uploader` works in Gerrit 3.14.1 (exact field name, required ACL, SOB trailer preservation) before implementing the rebase loop — reference Gerrit 3.9+ release notes where this parameter was added.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing to Phase 2's combined-chain batching design, add a Gerrit-specific prior art reference or a planned spike that validates cross-uploader relation-chain construction + ancestor-atomic FFO submit in Gerrit 3.14.1, explicitly addressing the documented Wikimedia FFO parallel-sibling limitation.
findings.finding: Phase 2's core batching mechanic — assembling multiple independent stacks (from different uploaders) into a single combined Gerrit relation chain, running one CI pass on the tip, then landing them ancestor-atomically — is labeled the 'Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains,' but Graphite and bors operate on GitHub Pull Requests, not Gerrit relation chains. The Gerrit-specific mechanics of cross-uploader combined-chain assembly (how to construct the combined chain, who owns each change after combination, `on_behalf_of` semantics across stacks, and whether Gerrit's submit ordering on the combined chain matches the batching intent) are novel on Gerrit and have no cited prior art or planned proof-of-concept.
findings.location: Phase 2 — batching AC: 'Cross-stack batching: assemble queued changes/stacks into one combined chain, run CI once on the tip, land them together if green (Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `RebaseInput.on_behalf_of_uploader` is supported on Gerrit 3.14.1 and probe whether the lander bot's service account requires a `forgeAuthor`/`forgeCommitter` ACL grant; document the required permission in the plan's security-invariant audit AC.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/trailers, but this `RebaseInput.on_behalf_of_uploader` field (Gerrit 3.6+ feature) has never been exercised in this codebase and is unverified against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance. If the field is absent, ignored, or requires a specific permission not in the current `Service Users` ACL, the lander will rebase as the bot identity — breaking DCO authorship and `rebar-ticket` trailer attribution.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service: 'on_behalf_of_uploader to preserve author/DCO/rebar-ticket trailers'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a SPIKE task: call `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}` from the bot account on a test change in the live 3.14.1 instance; check for 403 and verify the resulting patchset's author. If a `forgeAuthor` grant is required, add it to project.config (under refs/heads/* for Service Users) and note it in the security audit AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` (or its equivalent) exists and behaves correctly on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance; document the exact URL form and any required `RebaseInput` fields in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan cites `/rebase:chain` as the endpoint for rebasing a full relation chain, but this specific endpoint surface is unverified against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance and does not appear anywhere in the codebase or its existing scripts. The Gerrit REST API has `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with a `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` / chain option, and a separate `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` exists only in Gerrit ≥ 3.8 or as a plugin. A flag-level mismatch on this path will cause silent 404 failures at runtime.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase / /rebase:chain'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a SPIKE before committing Phase 2 scope: prove the batch-assembly mechanic (rebasing Stack B onto Stack A's tip and re-pushing to Gerrit under FFO) works end-to-end on the live 3.14.1 instance, and reconcile with the ADR-0040 'parallel sibling changes cannot be linearized under FFO' constraint.
findings.finding: Phase 2's core batching mechanic — programmatically assembling independent stacks into a single combined Gerrit relation chain, running one CI pass on the tip, and then performing an ancestor-atomic submit — is an entirely novel Gerrit operation not demonstrated anywhere in the codebase. The plan asserts this is achievable ('Graphite/bors model on Gerrit relation chains') without naming a concrete Gerrit REST path or proven mechanism for grafting two independent chains into one. This is a critical capability gap that could invalidate Phase 2 entirely.
findings.location: Phase 2 AC — 'assemble queued changes/stacks into one combined chain'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to S7 or S9 covering assembly-time 409: identify the conflicting stack(s), remove them from the batch (returning the whole conflicting stack to its agent with a needs_rebase outcome), and proceed with the remaining stacks.
findings.finding: Assembly-time inter-stack conflict (a 409 returned while rebasing one stack onto another during batch assembly) is owned by no child. S3 covers serial-mode 409 (Phase 1); S9 covers post-CI batch failure attribution; neither covers the case where batch assembly itself fails because two queued stacks conflict with each other.
findings.location: child 0128-9a17-79db-450e (S7) — Acceptance Criteria and Scope; child cf10-0423-e0de-464b (S9) — Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S9 (or S7): 'On assembly-time 409 (rebase conflict while assembling the combined chain): identify the conflicting stack at stack granularity, remove it from the batch, record a needs_rebase outcome for the whole conflicting stack (reusing the serial failure contract), and re-attempt assembly with the remaining stacks.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to S7 or S8 requiring re-verify of is:submittable on the exact tested SHA (combined tip) immediately before the Phase 2 batch atomic submit, consistent with the Phase 1 requirement.
findings.finding: No child includes an AC for re-verifying is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before the Phase 2 batch atomic submit. S2 covers this for Phase 1 (serial). S7 and S8 together cover the Phase 2 submit, but neither child's ACs require the re-verify-is:submittable check on the Phase 2 submit path.
findings.location: child 0128-9a17-79db-450e (S7) — AC3; child 23f7-b1b7-270e-4bed (S8) — Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S7 AC3: 'Immediately before POST /submit, re-verifies is:submittable on the combined tip SHA to guard against votes retracted between CI completion and submit.' Alternatively, add this to S8's security-invariant audit AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default timeout for 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' in the Phase 1 serial auto-lander AC (e.g., 'give up after 2 hours; remove Autosubmit and emit ci_failed') so the executor has a concrete default to implement.
findings.finding: The Phase 1 AC 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' specifies no timeout or maximum wait duration for CI completion, leaving an executor without a default behavior when CI is stuck, never reports, or the Verified vote never arrives.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service bullet: 'rebases to the current tip … → awaits a fresh Verified +1 → ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the serial auto-lander AC: 'If no Verified vote arrives within [e.g. 2 hours], remove Autosubmit, cancel the pending land, and return ci_failed to any waiting agent command.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the actor for 're-run CI on the isolated culprit stack' in the Phase 2 batch-failure AC (e.g., 'the bot automatically triggers a fresh CI run on the isolated culprit chain via the GHA API' or 'the culprit stack is handed back with needs_rebase outcome and the agent re-triggers CI manually').
findings.finding: The Phase 2 batch-failure AC specifies 're-run CI on the isolated culprit stack for a direct -1' but does not identify the actor — it is unclear whether the bot automatically triggers this re-run or whether a human/agent must do so manually.
findings.location: Phase 2 AC — Batch failure bullet: 'stack-granular attribution (re-run CI on the isolated culprit stack for a direct -1); hand the culprit stack back to its agent; re-batch the innocent stacks'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'wipChain' in the plan body (or replace with a self-explanatory term such as 'single serialized worker loop' or link to the Gerrit concept it references) so the AC is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The term 'wipChain' appears exactly once in the Phase 1 AC with no definition or cross-reference anywhere in the plan body, leaving implementers without a shared understanding of whether it means a Gerrit WIP-state chain, a serialization primitive, a lock name, or something else.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Single wipChain (serialized, one instance)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Single wipChain (serialized, one instance)' with a brief inline definition, e.g. 'Single serialized worker loop (one goroutine/process; no concurrent landing attempts)' — or add a parenthetical: 'Single wipChain (a serialization primitive ensuring only one landing attempt runs at a time)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope ADR-0042 (or a companion ADR-0043) to cover the Phase 2 gate-semantics change: the trust model for bot-cast `Verified` votes, why ancestor-atomic submit makes intermediate-state verification unnecessary, and the ACL design for the bot's Verified-vote authority.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 is scoped to 'auto-lander + agent contract' but Phase 2 introduces a materially different gate semantics (bot casts Verified from batch CI), which may not be adequately covered by an ADR whose stated scope is the landing mechanism and agent contract rather than gate security.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC: 'ADR-0042 records the auto-lander + agent contract'; Phase 2 AC: 'Gate-touching part (its own security-invariant-audit AC)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc-audit step to the Phase 1 AC: search all docs (ADRs, README, any onboarding guides) for references to the old manual rebase-retry landing workflow and update or add a forward-reference to ADR-0042.
findings.finding: The plan rewrites CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md landing sections but does not call for an audit of other documents (e.g., other ADRs, README, onboarding guides) that may reference the old manual landing workflow, risking stale cross-references.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC: 'Docs: rewrite this repo's landing section (CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that operationally tunable parameters (CI polling interval, rebase-retry backoff, Phase 2 batch assembly window, overall timeout) are exposed as environment variables or a config file in the service, not hardcoded, so they can be tuned without rebuilding the image.
findings.finding: The auto-lander service will have several operationally tunable parameters — polling interval for CI completion, rebase-retry backoff, Phase 2 batch assembly window (how long to wait for more changes before firing a batch), and overall timeout thresholds — but none are specified as configurable. These are values expected to evolve as real usage patterns emerge (burst size, CI duration, agent concurrency), and hardcoding them into the service will require code changes and redeployment each time they need tuning.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC: 'Serial auto-lander service'; Phase 2 AC: 'assemble queued changes/stacks into one combined chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the service design: 'All timing/threshold parameters (polling interval, batch window, max retries, timeout) are read from environment variables with documented defaults, configurable at compose-deploy time.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the auto-lander bot's Submit ACL: add the service account to the `Contributors` group (or a new `auto-lander` group granted submit), and document this in the `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` / `project.config` AC so it is provisioned alongside the `Autosubmit` label.
findings.finding: The Phase 1 serial auto-lander bot must call Gerrit's Submit API, which is currently restricted by `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` to the `Contributors` + `Administrators` groups in `project.config`. The plan specifies the bot gets a bot-only vote ACL for the new `Autosubmit` label but does not specify that the auto-lander service account also needs `Submit` permission — without which the bot can set `Autosubmit +1` but cannot actually submit the change, making the entire Phase 1 mechanism inoperable.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service … ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'; Phase 1 AC — 'Security-invariant audit: re-verify is:submittable on the exact tested SHA immediately before submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Phase 1 AC item: 'The auto-lander service account is added to the `Contributors` group (or a dedicated `auto-lander` group granted `submit` on `refs/heads/*`) via `setup-project.sh` so it can call the Gerrit Submit API; document the group membership in the security-invariant audit AC.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the security-invariant audit AC and the deployment AC: explicitly state that the lander-bot service account must be in BOTH `Service Users` (to cast `Autosubmit` votes) and `Contributors` (to submit changes), and add the group-membership setup step to `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` or `setup-project.sh`.
findings.finding: The plan does not state what auth credentials/ACL membership the new lander-bot service account requires beyond 'deploys like the review-bot.' To submit changes, the bot must be in the `Contributors` group (the `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` ACL in project.config is `group Contributors` only). To cast `Autosubmit` votes (bot-only ACL), it must be in `Service Users`. These are distinct groups with distinct setup steps, and neither is called out as an AC or deployment precondition.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — Serial auto-lander service + Security-invariant audit AC
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's Phase 1 AC under 'Security-invariant audit', add: 'lander-bot service account is a member of both `Service Users` (for label-Autosubmit vote ACL) and `Contributors` (for submit ACL); membership is converged by setup-project.sh.' This makes the auth preconditions explicit and auditable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare and add the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` ACL grant (scoped to `Service Users` group, mirroring the pattern for `label-Verified`) to `infra/gerrit/project.config` and include auditing this grant in the Phase 1 security-invariant audit AC.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `on_behalf_of_uploader` for the rebase REST call but does not declare or audit the Gerrit ACL (`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` permission on `refs/heads/*`) required to use it; the existing `project.config` grants no such permission to Service Users or any other group, leaving the lander bot's access posture for this privileged impersonation operation undeclared.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'rebases to the current tip … `on_behalf_of_uploader` to preserve author/DCO/`rebar-ticket` trailers'; Security-invariant audit AC; `infra/gerrit/project.config`
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/gerrit/project.config` under `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, add: `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader = group Service Users` (and `group Administrators` for symmetry). Add 'lander bot holds `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` on refs/heads/* scoped to Service Users' to the security-invariant audit AC checklist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly declare how the lander bot acquires Gerrit `submit` permission (e.g., a dedicated `rebar-lander-bot` group with a scoped submit grant, or a named account added to `Contributors`) in `project.config` and in the Phase 1 security-invariant audit AC, scoping the grant to the minimum required.
findings.finding: The lander bot must invoke the Gerrit submit action, but `project.config` restricts submit exclusively to `Contributors + Administrators` via `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`; the plan does not declare how the lander bot acquires submit permission, leaving an undeclared access posture on the most privileged operation in the flow.
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA'; `infra/gerrit/project.config` `[access "refs/heads/*"]` submit ACL; Security-invariant audit AC
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new `lander-bot` Gerrit group (analogous to the `feature-branch-drivers` pattern), add `submit = group lander-bot` alongside the existing Contributors/Administrators lines in `project.config`, and grant `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` to this group only — keeping the existing `Service Users` group out of the submit path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define `wipChain` in the Phase 1 AC or Context section: state whether it is an async lock/mutex, a queue, or a Gerrit WIP-label-based serialization, and describe its scope (per-repo? per-bot-instance?).
findings.finding: `wipChain` appears exactly once in the Phase 1 AC as a named mechanism ('Single `wipChain` (serialized, one instance)') but is never defined anywhere in the plan or codebase, leaving implementers without a shared understanding of what it is (a Gerrit Work-in-Progress chain? a concurrency lock? a queue data structure?).
findings.location: Phase 1 AC — 'Serial auto-lander service' bullet

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
